phase: PR-0 playable product recovery rebaseline; WM-0161 in progress; legacy WM-0154 through WM-0156 closeout semantics frozen pending roadmap reconciliation
activeMilestone: Formal playable product recovery roadmap: historical M0-M8 evidence is preserved, but product progress now requires one integrated authoritative GameSession, autonomous perceptible residents, real commands and input, authoritative save/load, and a reviewer-observed 30-minute vertical slice
lockedDecisions: TypeScript-first authoritative simulation in a dedicated Worker
PixiJS world rendering + React DOM UI + Electron Windows shell
Web remains a gated target; native macOS is deferred
Lantern / Chronicle / Obligation are the three product pillars
humanApprovalNeeded: Public release, Early Access launch, public Web launch or store submission
Signing, installer, updater or distribution beyond unsigned controlled external test
Telemetry, accounts, hosted services, paid services, crash upload or public feedback system
Final privacy, legal, store or public save compatibility commitments
Any post-M8 public release, release-candidate distribution or external commitment beyond the reviewed controlled-test/demo verdicts
notes: Task JSON, reports and inbox files remain the durable execution control plane. Historical M0-M8 closeouts preserve reviewed deterministic simulation, content, platform, localization, security, benchmark and scoped save evidence, but M8 is internal readiness evidence rather than a playable 1.0 product. Owner feedback and the 2026-07-10 product audit found that the default Web path still combines a static product-gate world with a two-pawn lamp/build Worker slice, lacks a continuously running integrated town, does not restore authoritative gameplay from save, and can pass synthetic canvas E2E while real viewport input remains blocked. WM-0161 therefore establishes PR-0 through PR-7 in docs/07_roadmap/12_playable_product_recovery_roadmap.md. WM-0154 is formally blocked by qa-performance, and WM-0155/WM-0156 must not close product readiness until a reviewed reconciliation aligns them with the new roadmap. WM-0157 through WM-0160 remain reusable Worker session/projection/drain infrastructure, but drain is not the normal game clock. After WM-0161 is verified and done, only WM-0162 may promote: it plans the integrated GameSession architecture and a limited PR-1 task DAG without implementation. Web remains demo-only; Windows remains unsigned ready-for-controlled-external-test; all public release, EA, store, signing, telemetry, account, paid-service and public save commitments remain Owner-gated.
